items.title: First Hari Raya without husband: Cop's widow focuses on children
items.summary: IPOH: For Nur Aini Samiun, Hari Raya Aidilfitri this year is marked by quiet resilience rather than celebration. The widow of the late Sergeant Mohd Roslan Abd Rahim, who died in a tragic Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) crash last year, said that the festive season felt starkly different without her husband of 15 years. I don't want them to feel sad because I am," she said.

items.title: Haj price still RM33,300: Tabung Haji locks in flight deals with Malaysia and Saudi Airlines, future might change if Gulf crisis persists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Pilgrims’ Fund Board (Tabung Haji–TH) has assured that the cost of performing the haj for the 1447H/2026 season will remain at RM33,300 despite global uncertainties. TH Group Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer Mustakim Mohamad said that although the ongoing conflict in West Asia could potentially impact costs, TH will absorb any unexpected increases. However, Mustakim said that TH may review future haj costs if the conflict in West Asia persists and significantly affects global oil prices.

items.title: Govt considers national committee to tackle illegal e-waste
items.summary: The Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry (NRES) said the proposal was discussed at the NRES Special Enforcement Task Force Meeting No. In a statement today, NRES said the meeting emphasised stronger enforcement on environmental issues, including the entry of illegal e-waste. NRES said the government remained committed to strengthening enforcement against environmental offences, including illegal e-waste.

items.title: MEF calls for swift government action as Middle East conflict hits businesses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Businesses are already feeling the pinch from the Middle East conflict with upward cost pressures in logistics, energy and raw materials, Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF) president Datuk Seri Syed Hussain Syed Husman said. He said the federation is concerned over emerging cost pressures and operational uncertainties faced by businesses amid the ongoing conflict. He said the situation highlights the vulnerability of Malaysian businesses, particularly micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), to external geopolitical shocks.

items.title: Penang Customs bust smuggling syndicate, seize RM3.9m worth of cigarettes, liquor
items.summary: AURIS FAQRURAZZI BUKIT MERTAJAM: Penang Customs has crippled a smuggling syndicate dealing in contraband cigarettes and liquor worth about RM3.9 million following a series of integrated operations carried out across the district last month. State Customs director Datuk Rohaizad Ali said the success stemmed from intelligence gathering and public tip-offs under Ops Petasan, which was conducted in stages and involved three separate raids on residential premises and a warehouse identified as storage sites for illicit goods. Rohaizad said in the first case, a raid was carried out on Feb 9 at a house in Kampung Sungai Lembu about 1.40pm.

items.title: For a safer Raya: Johor police to focus on 85 routes prone to crashes and jams statewide
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 18 — Johor police will monitor 47 hotspots (traffic congestion-prone areas) and 38 blackspots (accident-prone areas) throughout Ops Selamat 26 Hari Raya Aidilfitri, from tomorrow until Tuesday (March 24). Ab Rahaman said that Ops Selamat, themed “Kongsi Perjalanan, Kongsi Keselesaan, Keselamatan Diutamakan” (Share the Journey, Share Comfort, Safety First), focuses on three main objectives: preventing burglary, minimising road accidents and ensuring smooth traffic flow. Commenting on last year’s statistics, he said Ops Selamat recorded 1,642 accidents, with 26 fatalities, and issued 10,189 summonses for various traffic offences.

items.title: Alleged mastermind in Cikgu Chandra attack out on bail, police hunt for more suspects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Police have released on bail seven suspects, including the alleged mastermind, linked to the recent attack on activist S. Chandrasegaran, but are still hunting for several others involved in the case. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar confirmed today that the seven individuals who were most directly involved have been released, while the search for the remaining suspects continues, The New Straits Times reported. “The seven suspects who were previously detained have been released on police bail,” Shazeli said.

items.title: AI business blunders? Some insurers cautiously step up
items.summary: "The whole intent of using advanced AI is to substantially replace human assistance and oversight in decisions," said Phil Dawson, head of AI policy and partnerships at the specialist insurer Armilla. "That really challenges some of the fundamental logic of existing insurance coverage," Dawson said. Some standard insurance policies now include "absolute AI exclusion" clauses that expressly deny coverage for AI-related mishaps, Mitchell said.

items.title: Rice supply stabilising in Sabah interior districts
items.summary: KENINGAU: Rice supply in Sabah's interior has improved following increased stock distribution, State Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industries minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said. He said the monthly demand stands at 470 metric tonnes, with an average daily requirement of about 16 metric tonnes. "In Tenom alone, a total of 53 tonnes of rice has entered the retail market through Bernas-appointed distributors, namely PPK Tenom (30 tonnes) and Sazarice (23 tonnes).

items.title: Man sentenced to eight months’ imprisonment for damaging Sunway City temple
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — A man has been sentenced to eight months in prison after pleading guilty to desecrating a Chinese temple altar in Sunway City, Ipoh, last week. Perak police chief Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin confirmed the sentencing in a statement today. “A review of closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage found that an unidentified man had acted to damage the said temple on the previous night,” Noor Hisam said.

items.title: No queues, no chaos: Labuan ferry firms report smooth operations this Raya
items.summary: LABUAN, March 18 — The usually bustling atmosphere at Labuan’s two main ferry terminals ahead of Hari Raya Aidilfitri is noticeably absent this year, with both facilities reporting smooth operations and no signs of heavy congestion. Similarly, operations at the Labuan International Ferry Terminal appeared to be running as usual, with passenger boats maintaining regular schedules and no significant crowd of travellers seen at the terminal. Another operator, Labuan Mainland Link, is operating five daily trips using its ferries MV Goodwill Star, MV Putrajaya 1, MV Joystar and MV Kimanis 1 to cater to passenger demand.

items.title: Johor cops monitor 47 hotspots, 38 blackspots during Ops Selamat
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor police will monitor 47 hotspots (traffic congestion-prone areas) and 38 blackspots (accident-prone areas) throughout Ops Selamat 26 Hari Raya Aidilfitri, from tomorrow until Tuesday, March 24. Ab Rahaman said that Ops Selamat, themed "Kongsi Perjalanan, Kongsi Keselesaan, Keselamatan Diutamakan" (Share the Journey, Share Comfort, Safety First), focused on three main objectives: preventing burglary, minimising road accidents and ensuring smooth traffic flow. Commenting on last year's statistics, he said Ops Selamat recorded 1,642 accidents, with 26 fatalities, and issued 10,189 summonses for various traffic offences.

items.title: Iran's nuclear doctrine not likely to change, foreign minister says
items.summary: DUBAI: Iran's stance against the development of nuclear weapons won't significantly change, Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Al Jazeera in remarks relayed by Iranian media on Wednesday. However, he also cautioned that the new supreme leader is yet to publicly express his view on the matter. Former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed early in the US-Israeli war against Iran, opposed the development of weapons of mass destruction in a fatwa, or religious edict, issued in the early 2000s.

items.title: Nearly RM800k from Ramanan's salary, allowances channelled to his constituency
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sungai Buloh member of parliament Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has channelled nearly RM800,000 of his salary and allowances to his constituency since 2023. His latest contribution involves RM65,200 donated to mosques, suraus, temples, shrines and churches in the area. Ramanan has served as MP since 2022, after winning the Sungai Buloh seat for Pakatan Harapan in a seven-cornered fight.

items.title: Rawang family tragedy: Two killed in Kelantan road crash ahead of Hari Raya
items.summary: Syaherah Mustafa TANAH MERAH: A family of six from Rawang, Selangor, travelling to Kelantan to attend a relative's funeral, were struck by tragedy. Two members, Wan Rokiah Wan Ahmad, 64, and her granddaughter Nur Zara Zakirah Abdul Aliyy, 14, died at the scene after their vehicle collided along the Machang–Tanah Merah road near Bandar Baru Tanah Merah early this morning. The victims' nephew, Wan Mohd Azana Wan Mohd Najid, 53, said his aunt had travelled with her husband, her daughter who was driving, and three grandchildren to attend the funeral of her younger sister, Wan Nor Asmahani Wan Mohd Najid, who died of cancer two days ago.

items.title: ‘Hot spots’ or ‘black spots’, police zero in on Pahang’s 61 riskiest routes for festive travellers
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 18 — Pahang police have identified 36 accident-prone ‘black spots’ and 25 ‘hot spots’ statewide to be given priority attention during Ops Selamat, which runs from today until March 24. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the operation, held in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebrations, aimed at ensuring the safety of road users and reducing road accidents, including fatalities, in the state. Yahaya said the operation would also involve cooperation with various agencies, including the Fire and Rescue Department, Civil Defence Force (APM) and Road Transport Department (JPJ).

items.title: Cigarette butts top offences under new anti-littering law
items.summary: KUANTAN: Discarding cigarette butts in public places has emerged as the most common offence recorded since the country's new anti-littering law came into effect on Jan 1. Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Corporation (SWCorp) chief executive officer Khalid Mohamed said about 90 per cent of offences brought to court were related to littering cigarette butts. "During court proceedings, we noticed that the majority of offences involve throwing away cigarette butts.

items.title: Lorry driver remanded over alleged indecent exposure to underage supermarket cashier
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A lorry driver was remanded for six days after he allegedly exposed himself to an underage girl at a supermarket in Taman Berlian, Yong Peng, near here yesterday. He later returned to buy cigarettes, carrying a sling bag. While making payment at the counter, the suspect allegedly moved his sling bag aside and exposed himself.

items.title: Ports, factories, and illegal junk: Putrajaya gets serious on cleaning up Malaysia’s e-waste mess
items.summary: The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) said the proposal was discussed at the NRES Special Enforcement Task Force Meeting No. In a statement today, NRES said the meeting emphasised stronger enforcement on environmental issues, including the entry of illegal e-waste. NRES said the government remains committed to strengthening enforcement against environmental offences, including illegal e-waste.

items.title: City dwellers choose trains to avoid traffic congestion
items.summary: Student Hannah Nur Naimah Suhaimi, 21, said she had chosen public transport since her secondary school days due to connectivity factors that made it easier to access various locations. "I have been using public transport since secondary school until now. "I have been using public transport since I was 10 years old during my schooling days in Kampung Baru.

items.title: Friday or Monday? Sabah sets extra Raya public holiday based on Syawal date
items.summary: According to a State Government Gazette issued today, Governor Tun Musa Aman has declared Friday (March 20) as an additional public holiday if Aidilfitri falls on Saturday (March 21). The gazette also stated that if Aidilfitri is instead declared on Friday, the replacement public holiday will be observed on Monday (March 23). Last Sunday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced a similar arrangement for additional Aidilfitri public holidays nationwide to facilitate travel and planning for the public.

items.title: Eastbound traffic from Klang Valley builds up ahead of Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic from the Klang Valley heading to the east coast began to build up this morning as travellers started returning to their hometowns ahead of Aidilfitri. Traffic flow to the north and south remained smooth, with only minor congestion reported between Bandar Baharu and Jawi due to a broken-down lorry. "LLM expected traffic volume to increase further from midday following the 50 per cent toll discount, which began at midnight and will run until 11.59 pm tomorrow," he told Bernama when contacted.

items.title: Op Selamat 26: Johor police step up enforcement at 85 locations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Johor police have deployed 2,018 officers and personnel for the Hari Raya festive period, with enforcement focused on 85 identified congestion and accident-prone locations statewide. State police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the manpower mobilisation under Op Selamat 26 aims to meet three key objectives: curbing house break-ins, reducing road accidents, and ensuring smooth traffic flow. He said the operation would run from tomorrow until March 24, involving 69 officers and 663 personnel from the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department, as well as 168 officers and 1,118 personnel from other units within the Johor contingent.

items.title: The bamboo journey behind Batu Pahat's Lemang Bodong craze
items.summary: Lemang Bodong operator Mohammad Amril Azami said that quality bamboo sourced from Pahang and Hulu Langat, Selangor, remained the defining factor in producing the traditional delicacy at its best. The best medium-grade bamboo is found in Pahang," he said when met at Kampung Parit Ju, Jalan Parit Jalil. To meet festive demand, Mohammad Amril said he typically ordered about 7,500 bamboo sticks to produce 7,000 lemang, allowing for losses from cracks or damage during cooking.

items.title: DAP leaders publicly rebuke own deputy minister over appearance with activist Arun
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Four DAP leaders today publicly rebuked Deputy Minister M. Kulasegaran for his presence at a court complex with an activist facing charges, saying the action invited “public controversy” and does not represent the party’s official stance. In a joint statement, DAP central executive committee (CEC) members Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali, Young Syefura Othman, and Syahredzan Johan, along with Bukit Bendera MP Syerleena Abdul Rashid, said they were in “complete disagreement” with their fellow party veteran’s actions. They said that Kulasegaran should have been more mindful of how his actions would be seen, given his official role as a deputy minister in the Prime Minister's Department.

items.title: Jualan Termurah Madani: 2,000 chickens sold out in just 30 minutes
items.summary: Bukit Gantang: About 2,000 chickens, priced at RM7 per kg, were snapped up within just half an hour today at the Jualan Termurah Madani programme held at the Bukit Gantang member of parliament Service Centre in conjunction with Aidilfitri celebrations. Azhari Abd Aziz, 63, from Kampung Hujung Matang, said he took the opportunity to buy 40 chickens to distribute to the less fortunate in his village. Meanwhile, Bukit Gantang member of parliament Datuk Syed Abu Hussin Hafiz Syed Abdul Fasal said the bulk-purchase approach was implemented to provide lower prices to the public without the need for government subsidies.

items.title: The math is mathing: 2,000 chickens, RM7 per kilo, gone in 30 minutes at Bukit Gantang Raya sale
items.summary: BUKIT GANTANG, March 18 — About 2,000 chickens, priced at RM7 per kilogram, were snapped up within just half an hour today at the Jualan Termurah Madani programme held at the Bukit Gantang MP Service Centre in conjunction with Aidilfitri celebrations. Apart from chickens, eager shoppers also seized the chance to buy beef at as low as RM24 per kilogram, along with cooking oil, eggs, flour, sugar and other essential items in preparation for Aidilfitri. Meanwhile, Bukit Gantang MP Datuk Syed Abu Hussin Hafiz Syed Abdul Fasal said the bulk-purchase approach was implemented to provide lower prices to the public without the need for government subsidies.

items.title: Afghans search for loved ones at Kabul rehab centre bombed by Pakistan
items.summary: KABUL: Families and friends of people who were under treatment at a rehab centre in the Afghan capital Kabul searched for their loved ones on Wednesday, two days after it was bombed by Pakistan in the deadliest incident in the months long conflict between the neighbours. The Afghan Taliban government has said that more than 400 people were killed and 265 wounded in the air strike that took place on Monday night, just as people and staff at the centre were praying, days before the end of the holy month of Ramadan. "We came here looking for our patient, he is missing," said Mazar, 50, who gave only one name.

items.title: Pahang police focus on 36 black spots, 25 hot spots during Ops Selamat
items.summary: KUANTAN: Pahang police have identified 36 accident-prone 'black spots' and 25 'hot spots' statewide for priority attention during Ops Selamat, which runs from today until March 24. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the operation, held in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebrations, aimed to ensure the safety of road users and reduce road accidents, including fatalities, in the state. In addition, duties such as patrols and security control were intensified," he said after officiating the handing-over ceremony of the Pahang Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department chief at the Pahang Police Contingent Headquarters here today.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police tracking several suspects in Cikgu Chandra intimidation case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are still tracking down several people to assist in investigations into a case of mischief and criminal intimidation at a residential area in Taman Kinrara, Puchong, last week. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said several suspects are still being pursued to assist in the investigation. "The seven suspects who were previously detained have been released on police bail," Shazeli said when contacted today.

items.title: Man fined RM1,500 for injuring ex-fiancée
items.summary: Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid imposed the fine on Syed Zamri Syed Zainuddin after he pleaded guilty to intentionally causing injury to Putri Fasha Felisa Mohd Izhan, 19, on Feb 21. He also ordered Syed Zamri to serve four months' imprisonment in default of payment. Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Syamsul Ikhmal Ramli prosecuted, while the accused was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation lawyer Nik Nazriani Nik Ab Rahman.

items.title: Man held for allegedly filming woman in supermarket toilet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A man was arrested for allegedly recording a video of a woman in a supermarket toilet in Miri, Sarawak, reports said. According to a report by the Borneo Post, Miri police said the suspect was arrested at the Permy Technology Park about 2pm on Monday. Police also said they viewed seriously any action that infringes on privacy and threatens public safety, particularly involving women in public spaces.

items.title: Tetra Pak, Aeon Mall partner for nationwide carton recycling drive
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Global food processing and packaging solutions provider Tetra Pak has launched its Sort & Drop – Recycle the Cartons programme, with plans to roll it out to 27 Aeon malls over the next three years. She said while the initiative will expand to 27 Aeon malls, the model will also be opened to other malls following the launch. For example, Aeon involves 27 malls over three years, which averages about nine malls annually.

items.title: Sabah announces additional public holiday for Hari Raya
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The state government has announced an additional public holiday in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidilfitri. In a statement, the Chief Minister's Department said the holiday would fall on either March 20 or March 23. Under a Special Government Gazette dated March 18, 2026, the Yang di-Pertua Negeri (Head of State) designated the following as additional public holidays — March 20, 2026, if Hari Raya Aidilfitri falls on March 21, 2026, or March 23, 2026, if Hari Raya Aidilfitri falls on March 20, 2026.

items.title: Jobless man who just completed jail sentence charged over spanner attack
items.summary: Adam Muhammad is accused of voluntarily causing grievous harm to a 30-year-old victim, known only as Sani, with a spanner that is about 40cm long, a weapon which could have caused the victim's death. Adam entered his plea before judge Dr Azrol Abdullah. Deputy public prosecutor Vivien Yeap Jie Xi opposed bail.

items.title: Trump's summit delay casts pall over US-China trade truce
items.summary: US President Donald Trump's request to delay the planned summit in Beijing with Chinese leader Xi Jinping casts a shadow over mutual ties that have been stable since their last meeting in October, but is unlikely to cause a major setback, analysts said. Trump's move shows how the Iran conflict has upended his foreign policy agenda and added war to trade and Taiwan among the spectrum of issues separating the world's two biggest economies. The negotiations followed Washington's imposition of new investigations into "unfair trade practices" aimed at rebuilding trade pressure on countries around the world - including China - after the US Supreme Court struck down Trump's global tariffs last month.

items.title: Price alignment safeguards supply, curbs leakages
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aligning fuel prices with actual market costs is critical to ensuring supply stability, as Malaysia does not function in isolation from global markets. He said maintaining retail prices that are not aligned with market costs could increase fiscal pressure. "When retail prices are set far below actual market costs, the burden on government subsidies rises significantly and tends to create harmful market responses, such as excessive purchasing and smuggling.

items.title: Nilai urn theft: Suspect demands ransom via WhatsApp for 30 missing remains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The case of 30 missing urns from a memorial park in Nilai has evolved into an extortion investigation after a suspect contacted the management demanding payment for the return of the cremated remains, police said. Nilai district police chief Supt Johari Yahya confirmed that the suspect reached out via WhatsApp using a foreign number, claiming to have found the urns. “The suspect then made a payment demand as a condition to return the remains,” Johari said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: HCK Capital taps Binastra for Subang Sentral revival
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: HCK Capital Group Bhd has appointed Binastra Builders Sdn Bhd as the main contractor to complete the long-stalled Subang Sentral development. Binastra Builders is a wholly owned subsidiary of Binastra Corp Bhd. "We are excited to collaborate with Binastra in bringing Subang Sentral back to life.

items.title: Haj cost stays put: TH maintains RM33,300 fee despite oil surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) expects the haj cost for the 1447 Hijrah season to remain unchanged at RM33,300, despite rising global oil prices. Group managing director and chief executive officer Mustakim Mohamad said the cost stability is supported by existing agreements with airlines, which have already factored in potential fuel price fluctuations. "We have engagements with two airlines, namely Malaysia Airlines and Saudi Airlines, where the framework agreement already accounts for any increase in oil prices," he said at a press conference today.

items.title: Top Glove's Q2 net profit up 1.6pct to RM30.76mil on RM1.0bil revenue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Top Glove Corp Bhd's second-quarter net profit grew 1.6 per cent to RM30.76 million from RM30.28 million a year ago, driven by higher sales orders across most regions. Revenue for the quarter ended Feb 28, 2026 also rose 14 per cent to RM1 billion from RM883.65 million last year, according to a bourse filing. Top Glove said the Middle East conflict has given rise to global crude oil supply disruption, which affects the availability of nitrile latex, a key raw material for nitrile gloves.

items.title: Umbrellas up: MetMalaysia sounds storm warning for KL, Selangor, eight states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a fresh weather alert for several regions across the country, with heavy rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds expected to persist until 5pm today. According to the advisory issued at 2.15pm, the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia include the entire Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur as well as the Selangor districts of Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, and Petaling. In East Malaysia, the weather warning covers Sri Aman, Kapit (Song and Kapit), and Miri (Telang Usan) in Sarawak.

items.title: Indonesian man charged with causing teen’s death while carrying sickle in car
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — An Indonesian man was charged in the Teluk Intan Magistrate's Court today with two counts of dangerous driving, including one for causing the death of a teenager last week while allegedly carrying a long-handled sickle. The accused, Sutiyono, 39, who is hearing-impaired, nodded to show he understood the charges read before Magistrate Naidatul Athirah Azman, Berita Harian reported. For the first and more serious charge, Sutiyono is accused of causing the death of 18-year-old Adham Faisal through dangerous driving.

items.title: Tabung Haji posts highest profit in eight years, 3.5pc payout to benefit 9.7 million depositors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Pilgrims’ Fund Board (Tabung Haji -TH) today announced a profit distribution of 3.50 per cent, after zakat, for the 2025 financial year, the highest rate recorded since 2018. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), Zulkifli Hasan, said the total profit distribution amounted to RM3.22 billion, benefiting more than 9.7 million TH depositors. Also present were TH Group Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer Mustakim Mohamad, TH Chairman Abdul Rashid Hussain, and TH Finance Department Group Executive Director Shamsul Bahar Shamsudin.

items.title: Penang police: Baby found unconscious at Seberang Jaya daycare died of asphyxiation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — A four-month-old baby boy who died while under the care of a daycare centre near Seberang Jaya Hospital last week was asphyxiated, police confirmed today based on initial autopsy findings. Penang Police Chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said investigation papers are nearly complete and will soon be referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor for further action, Sinar Harian reported. The victim, identified as Hayl Haarith Mohd Syafaeez, was found unconscious at the 24-hour daycare, located within the hospital compound, in the early hours of last Monday.

items.title: Businesses need ready cash relief, wait-and-see approach could hurt MSMEs if Gulf crisis deepens, employers tells govt
items.summary: President Datuk Dr Syed Hussain Syed Husman, while acknowledging the government’s cautious approach, called for preparedness and flexibility in policy response should the situation in West Asia deteriorate. However, the government must remain ready to act swiftly if conditions worsen,” he said in a statement today. Syed Hussain said the situation reinforces the need for businesses to accelerate productivity enhancements, diversify supply chains, and adopt technology to mitigate future disruptions.

items.title: JKR projects see mixed progress, Kuching complex leads the way with one-third on track
items.summary: KUCHING, March 18 — About one-third of Public Works Department (JKR) projects nationwide are currently progressing ahead of schedule, said Deputy Works Minister Ahmad Maslan. He said that of the 855 ongoing projects, roughly one-third are either ahead of time or on track, while the remainder are behind schedule. Those that are behind schedule must be prioritised,” he said after inspecting the Kuching Federal Multipurpose Building Complex (BGP) construction site today.

items.title: Sarawak government aligns with Putrajaya, declares additional Hari Raya holiday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Sarawak government has officially confirmed that it will observe the additional public holiday for Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026, aligning with the recent announcement made by the federal government. The statement clarified that if Hari Raya falls on Friday, March 20, the additional holiday will be observed on the following Monday, March 23. However, if the first day of Syawal is on Saturday, March 21, then the preceding Friday, March 20, will be the public holiday.

items.title: Murder charge for Melaka man over fatal attack on associate in Sungai Udang, case set for April 29
items.summary: MELAKA, March 18 — A man was charged at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here today with murdering his acquaintance. Saiful Hadi Othman, 44, is charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code with the murder of Muhamad Hanafi Yusof, 25, at a house in Sungai Udang here, between 7.58 pm and 8.15 pm on March 11 this year. Deputy Public Prosecutor Syaza Nur Sharif prosecuted, while Saiful Hadi was unrepresented.

items.title: Race against the clock: Indira Gandhi launches contempt action against IGP before missing daughter turns 18
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — In a final and desperate bid to recover her long-abducted daughter, M. Indira Gandhi has initiated contempt of court proceedings against the Inspector-General of Police for the force’s decade-long failure to execute a court order to locate the child. The legal move comes with immense urgency, as Indira’s daughter, Prasana Diksa, is set to turn 18 early next month. Indira’s lawyer, Rajesh Nagarajan, said the action was taken due to the “lackadaisical attitude” of the police, Free Malaysia Today reported.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan MB says following Putrajaya’s lead, cancels state Raya open house to save cost
items.summary: SEREMBAN, March 18 — The Negeri Sembilan state government has cancelled its Aidilfitri open house this year in line with a federal decision, as part of cost-saving measures. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said the event scheduled to take place in Bandar Sri Jempol on April 4, as well as the national-level Madani Aidilfitri celebration on April 12, will not proceed. He said the decision reflects a prudent approach to managing the state’s finances.

items.title: Ex-HR minister Steven Sim touts pro-worker record after High Court rules he was ‘biased’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR — Former human resources minister Steven Sim has defended his pro-worker record, following a High Court ruling that found he acted with bias in favour of employers during a trade dispute last year. Sim reiterated his position, saying his track record in government has always been to support workers, Free Malaysia Today reported. When asked about the ruling, Sim, declined to comment due to the ongoing legal process.

items.title: Does the AG have immunity? Najib’s launches final bid to revive suit against Tommy Thomas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak has taken his RM1.9 million lawsuit against former attorney general Tan Sri Tommy Thomas to the Federal Court in a final bid to revive the case. The move comes after the Court of Appeal in January upheld a High Court decision to strike out the suit, which alleges misfeasance in public office and malicious prosecution, Free Malaysia Today reported Najib sued Thomas in 2020 over charges brought against him related to 1MDB, seeking RM1.9 million in special damages to cover his legal defence preparation costs. In a significant finding, however, the appellate court disagreed with the High Court on one key point: the immunity of the attorney general.

items.title: Free Go KL bus to Cheras Rehab Hospital from April 1, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh today announced that the free Go KL bus service will be extended to the Cheras Rehabilitation Hospital (HRC) from April 1, in a move aimed at improving access to public healthcare, particularly for the elderly and those with mobility challenges. She said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) will expand the GOKL11 route – which runs between MRT Maluri and Bandar Tun Razak – by adding a new stop within the hospital grounds. She said the bus service will provide much needed access to the public.

items.title: Penang JPJ ramps up Raya patrols in road safety push, aims to cut accidents by 10pc
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, March 18 — The Penang Road Transport Department (JPJ) is intensifying its advocacy programmes during Ops Hari Raya Aidilfitri (HRA) 2026, aiming to reduce road accidents by 10 per cent throughout the festive season. State JPJ director Zulkifly Ismail said more face-to-face engagements with drivers are being carried out to boost the Perception of Being Caught (POBC) among road users. He said eight key areas have been identified as frequent accident zones, including Jalan Teluk Air Tawar to Jalan Bagan Luar and the North-South Expressway in North Seberang Perai; Jalan Perusahaan Perai in Central Seberang Perai; Jalan Besar Simpang Ampat in South Seberang Perai; Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu Expressway and Jalan Paya Terubong in the Northeast; as well as Jalan Tun Dr Awang and Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah in the Southwest.

items.title: Tick-tock trouble: Domestic trade officers seize knock-off luxe watches worth nearly RM800,000 in Putrajaya
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 18 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) seized 790 units of counterfeit branded watches, worth an estimated RM790,600, during a raid in the federal capital on March 13. KPDN Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the raid was conducted by the Putrajaya Enforcement Division following a nearly month-long investigation into premises suspected of storing and distributing the fake watches. Azman said the counterfeit watches were believed to have been sourced from overseas suppliers before being sold to local customers.

items.title: Anwar wins bid to strike out legal challenge over PM and Tambun MP posts, judge rules Waytha Moorthy has no case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The High Court here today allowed Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s bid to strike out an originating summons filed by P. Waytha Moorthy challenging his appointment as Prime Minister and Tambun Member of Parliament (MP). Judge Alice Loke Yee Ching, in allowing the application, held that the court has no jurisdiction to entertain the summons, as any challenge to an election must be brought by way of an election petition. Justice Loke further said that the court, in hearing the originating summons, was not sitting as an election court, nor was the action commenced by way of an election petition.

items.title: Hire-purchase changes aim to protect borrowers, little impact on banks, says economist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The introduction of key amendments to hire-purchase financing practices under the Hire-Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026 (HPAA) marks a significant move in strengthening borrowers’ protection, particularly for those looking to sell vehicles before their financing contracts mature, an economist said. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the changes would benefit borrowers who intend to trade in or dispose of their vehicles early, as the reducing balance method offers greater savings than the Rule of 78, which front-loads interest at the beginning of the loan tenure. He said to some degree, it could help improve purchasers’ affordability.

items.title: Accidental tick? Marking error won’t hold up 2025 income tax refunds, IRB assures taxpayers
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 18 — Individual taxpayers do not need to submit an amended Income Tax Return Form (BNCP) for the Year of Assessment 2025 to correct an accidental error in marking the “Tax Borne by Employer” column. “The IRB has noted that some individual taxpayers inadvertently marked the ‘Tax borne by employer’ column when filling out their Income Tax Return Forms for the Year of Assessment 2025, resulting in delays in receiving excess tax refunds. “For cases confirmed as marking errors, the excess tax refunds will be processed in stages,” the statement said The IRB explained that the tax refund system operates automatically based on the information provided by taxpayers in their returns.

items.title: ‘Balik kampung’ traffic from Klang Valley: East coast-bound jams start early
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Traffic from the Klang Valley heading to the east coast began to build up this morning as travellers started returning to their hometowns ahead of Aidilfitri. “LLM expects traffic volume to increase further from midday following the 50 per cent toll discount, which began at midnight and will run until 11.59 pm tomorrow,” he told Bernama when contacted. — Bernama
